seo_title: What Makes Work Valuable According to Michal Masny
meta_description: Michal Masny, NC Ethics of Technology Postdoctoral Fellow, discusses the value of work and its impact on well-being.
body_html:
Work is both necessary and positively valuable, according to Michal Masny. He argues that our lives might be worsened if we were to eliminate work completely, but there can be optimal combinations of work and leisure time.

Develop excellence at something Make a social contribution Gain social recognition Create and sustain community

Challenges to Traditional Education

The traditional division of labor is that scientists and engineers invent new technologies, and then philosophers and lawyers evaluate and regulate them. GroupRole Scientists and EngineersInvent new technologies Philosophers and LawyersEvaluate and regulate technologies

Redefining the Role of Philosophers in Technology “I want to create scientists who think about their projects and potential outcomes as lawyers and philosophers might, and vice versa,” says Masny.

The Importance of Eliminating the ‘Wisdom Gap’

Citing scientist Carl Sagan’s warning about the dangers of becoming “powerful without becoming commensurately wise” The pace at which new technologies are invented and deployed has made this division of labor unworkable.

source.title: A philosophy of work
source.summary: As the NC Ethics of Technology Postdoctoral Fellow, Michal Masny is advancing dialogue, teaching, and research into the social and ethical dimensions of new computing technologies.
